synopsis:
The story opens with a meeting between Talon Karrde and Luke and Mara Jade Skywalker. Karrde's people had intercepted an urgent transmission addressed to Luke, sent from Admiral Voss Parck on the planet Nirauan, but the message was stolen by one of Karrde's own operatives, Dean Jinzler. Fearing the transmission might be connected to the unknown menace Parck and Baron Soontir Fel had warned them of years before, Luke and Mara travel to Nirauan.

There they learn the message actually came from the Chiss Aristocra Chaf'orm'bintrano, called Formbi. The Chiss have located the remains of *Outbound Flight*, the pioneering Jedi expedition destroyed decades earlier by Grand Admiral Thrawn, and wish to hand their find over to the New Republic. Luke and Mara join an odd delegation aboard the *Chaf Envoy*: a squad of stormtroopers, a remnant of an alien people called the Geroons who claim a debt to those aboard Outbound Flight, and a man posing as a New Republic ambassador. As they voyage deeper into Chiss space through a treacherous star cluster known as the Redoubt, mounting sabotage and theft deepen the Skywalkers' unease, and Mara feels torn between her loyalty to the New Republic and her respect for the Imperial Remnant.

When the expedition reaches the wreck of Outbound Flight, Mara senses life aboard: survivors, perhaps hundreds of them. The supposedly peaceful Geroons are then revealed to be the bloodthirsty Vagaari, bent on revenge against the Chiss and Outbound Flight for their defeat by Thrawn. Luke, Mara, the Chiss, the Imperials, and the embittered survivors must fight together to live. Though they defeat the boarding force, the Vagaari sabotage the *Chaf Envoy* and seize one of the derelict Dreadnaughts, striking back through the Redoubt toward the Chiss command center. Recovering a Delta-Twelve Skysprite, Luke and Mara pursue the rogue ship, board it, and use a false signal to trick the main Vagaari carrier before turning the captured Dreadnaught against the enemy fleet and helping the Chiss win the day. The survivors are entrusted to the Empire of the Hand, and Luke and Mara are left free to settle into their marriage, though Mara cannot shake the feeling that the whole affair was too neatly orchestrated, as if Thrawn, a master manipulator, might have prepared other clones of himself hidden across the Unknown Regions.
title: Survivor's Quest
